week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782 (standalone halachic guide, no weekly zmanim block)
occasion: Nine Days begin (Rosh Chodesh Av); fast postponed since 9 Av is Shabbos
label: NOTE
verbatim: All the restrictions and limitations of the "Nine Days" commence on Rosh Chodesh (this year on Thurs evening 28 July) and continue until after the end of the actual fast. This year, with the date of 9 (Tisha) b'Av being on a Shabbos, the fast is postponed to 10 Av.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Nine Days - laundering/mikveh allowance on Rosh Chodesh Av Friday and Erev Shabbos Chazon
label: NOTE
verbatim: A person who washes with hot water and/or toivels himself in the mikveh every Friday may wash with hot water without soap (others do permit soap) and toivel himself on this Friday (Rosh Chodesh Av) and also next Friday, Erev Shabbos Devarim/ Chazon.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Motzaei Shabbos 2 Av - Havdalah given to a child
label: NOTE
verbatim: On motzaei Shabbos 2 Av (this year 30 July) we make Havdalah on wine, but we give the wine to a child who is mature enough to appreciate the importance of making a brocha before drinking, but is not mature enough to appreciate the obligation to mourn over Yerushalayim. If there is no such child available, the person making Havdalah should drink the wine.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Shabbos Chazon (9 Av) - Torah study restrictions from chatzos
label: NOTE
verbatim: Shabbos Devarim, Chazon: this year it is on 9 Av: All the restrictions that apply to Torah study on a regular erev Tisha b'Av apply on this Shabbos as well, from chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) onwards. We may study only the books of Eichah and Iyov, the unfavourable prophecies in the book of Yirmeyahu, Midrash Eichah and the description of the churban as recorded in the Talmud Maseches Gittin 55b. The activities prohibited on Tisha b'Av that are private in nature are prohibited on this Shabbos as well.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Shabbos before the Fast - seudah shlishis allowance
label: NOTE
verbatim: We may eat meat and drink wine at the meal on Shabbos afternoon before the Fast. However, one should not feast with company.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Eating cutoff before the Fast begins
label: Eating cutoff (shkia)
verbatim: All eating and drinking must be finished by shkia (sunset), this year at 5:19pm. Benching may be after shkia.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Motzaei Shabbos 9 Av - start of Fast observance
label: Motzaei Shabbos marker
verbatim: At motzaei Shabbos 9 Av (5:58pm), say "Baruch hamavdil bein kodesh l'chol". Then put on non-leather shoes.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
label: Maariv (seating note)
verbatim: In the synagogue or at home for Maariv, we sit on low chairs/stools or cushions. The shule furniture is not suitable and it will not be available, so bring your own.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Tisha b'Av - beracha over a flame after Maariv
label: NOTE
verbatim: After Maariv, we say the beracha "…Borei me'orei ha'esh" over a flame. However, Havdalah is not said.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Tisha b'Av - Megillas Eichah and Kinos after Maariv
label: Megillah/Kinos
verbatim: We continue to sit on low chairs or cushions and read Megillas Eichah and selected Kinos, then say "Ve'Atah Kadosh…", a full kaddish, Aleinu and kiddush yatom.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
label: NOTE
verbatim: In the morning of the Fast day (this year on 10 Av / Sun 7 Aug), upon rising from sleep, a person should wash only his fingers, and not the entire hand. If he has sleep around his eyes, and he would normally wash it off, he may do so on this occasion as well. We do not say the beracha "…she'asah li kal tzarki" until the next morning.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
label: Shacharis (Fast day)
verbatim: Shacharis: no tallis or tefillin, and we do not kiss the tzizis of the tallis katan. We say Kinos after the Haftorah. It is customary for each person to say Megillas Eichah individually after the Kinos. We do not say the Tehillim of the day or "Ein Kelokenu…" until the afternoon.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
label: NOTE
verbatim: Until chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) we don't do any work that involves some time or concentration. Similarly, we do not sit on regular chairs or prepare food for the evening meal until after chatzot hayom.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Fast day - tallis/tefillin resume, Mincha Gedolah
label: Mincha (Fast day)
verbatim: Before Mincha: from chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) and onwards we may put on tallis and tefillin saying the appropriate berachos. Then we say the Shema, shir haYom, Ein k'elokeinu, the portion of Tehillim for the day and any shiurim which normally follow Shacharis. That concludes the omissions made in Shacharis. From Mincha Gedolah (this year 12:29pm) we may start saying the korbonos for Mincha, then continue with Krias haTorah and Haftorah, followed by Shmonei Esrei etc.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Fast day - Rabbeinu Tam tefillin after Mincha
label: After Mincha (Fast day)
verbatim: After Mincha: we put on Rabbeinu Tam tefillin and say the usual passages.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
label: Maariv / end of Fast / Havdalah
verbatim: Ending the Fast: at 5:46pm. We daven Maariv, wash negel vasser (no beracha), wash our faces and rinse our mouths. We can wear leather shoes again. We make Havdalah over wine: we say "…Borei pri hagafen" and the Havdalah beracha, then drink the wine. We don't use a candle or spices.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: End of the Fast - Kiddush Levanah
label: Kiddush Levanah
verbatim: Make Kiddush Levanah.

week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
occasion: Meat/wine restriction continues until 11 Av morning
label: NOTE
verbatim: We do not eat meat or drink wine until the morning of 11 Av (Mon 8 Aug). However, the other restrictions of the Nine Days are now removed.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: Mincha
verbatim: Mincha: 4:00pm.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: Purim Seudah deadline
verbatim: Purim Seudah: We are required to make a feast during the day of Purim. It is customary to have eaten the main part of the meal by Shkia (7:01pm).

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: Maariv
verbatim: Maariv: at Tzemach Tzedek, starting at 7:30pm.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: NOTE - Megillah brochos/reading practice
verbatim: Megillah reading at Maariv and Shacharis is preceded by three brochos: al mikrah Megillah, she'asah nissim, and Shehecheyanu. When saying Shehecheyanu at Shacharis, one should also have in mind the mitzvos of mishloach manos, matanos l'evyonim and the Purim seudah...

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: NOTE - Mishloach Manos / Purim donation cards
verbatim: Mishloach manos: On the day of Purim, every person is required to send two different foods to one other Jewish person of the same gender. Instead of spending many hours buying, packaging and delivering Mishloach Manos to numerous people, Tzemach Tzedek has made available 'PURIM DONATION CARDS' which you can send to people...

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: NOTE - Matanos La'evyonim / Krinsky's
verbatim: Matanos La'evyonim: Every person is to give one present to each of two poor people. That is the minimum requirement... Matanos La'evyonim can be arranged with Dovid Krinsky at Krinsky's Kosher Grocery, for distribution to those in need.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: NOTE - no tachanun
verbatim: No tachanun or tachanun-related sections in the davening.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: NOTE - add to joy
verbatim: A person should add slightly to his joy and celebration during the meals of this day.

week_context: Details and Notes for Purim 5784 (2024)
label: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana
verbatim: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana for Adar II this year is on 15 Adar II at 4:35am Jerusalem time (Mon. 25/3 at 1:35pm Sydney Daylight Saving time).

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Purim falls Friday, seudah earlier
verbatim: Note that because Purim day is a Friday this year, we make the seudah much earlier than in other years.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - avodah message
verbatim: Purim is not so much about ancient history, as about current life today and our avodah here and now. This truth is so important to actualise, that if someone reads the Megillah thinking of the events only as ancient history, it is questionable if they have fulfilled their obligation to read. So, don't be caught with your head in the wrong era!

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Parashas Zochor reading
verbatim: On the Shabbos prior to Purim, we read from two Sifrei Torah. The weekly Parsha (Tetzaveh) is read from the first sefer Torah scroll. The second sefer Torah is used for reading Parshas Zochor, which is found at the end of Parshas Ki Setze...

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Fast begins/ends
verbatim: All men (over 13 years old) and women (over 12 years old) should fast – refraining from eating and drinking on this day, from daybreak (5:33am) until nightfall (7:42pm). Pregnant or nursing women, or people feeling ill, are exempt. Consult a Rov if necessary.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - fast leniency until Megillah
verbatim: One must fast until after Maariv and the reading of the Megillah are completed. However, if one is weak, one may drink and eat before the Megillah. In that case, one should avoid eating more than a kezayis of cake or bread before Megillah.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Shacharis minyonim
verbatim: Minyonim for Shacharis will be at 6:15am, 7:30am and 9:00am.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Aneinu/Tachanun/Selichos
verbatim: The Shliach Tzibur says Aneinu in the repetition of the Shemoneh Esrei. As on all Fast days, Tachanun, Selichos and Avinu Malkeinu are recited.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Machatzis Hashekel (detailed)
verbatim: It is customary to give three coins to tzedakah on Ta'anis Esther before Mincha, to recall the half-shekel that was given annually to the Temple treasury in the month of Adar when the Beis HaMikdash was standing...

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Mincha (no Tachanun, Erev Purim)
verbatim: We read the usual Torah and Haftorah for a Fast day. We say Aneinu in Shemoneh Esrei in Shema Koleinu. We do not say Tachanun or Avinu Malkeinu at Minchah, because it is Erev Purim.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Purim on Friday / five mitzvos
verbatim: Many have the minhag to wear Shabbos clothing on Purim from the evening beginning Purim, because it is a festive day. All men and women, as well as children who have reached the age of chinuch, are obligated in the five major mitzvos of Purim: 1. Listen to the reading of the Megillah 2. Say Al HaNisim in each Shemoneh Esrei and Birchas HaMazon 3. Send Mishloach Manos 4. Give Matanos L'Evyonim 5. Eat the festive Purim meal

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Al HaNisim in Maariv
verbatim: Maariv – Say Al HaNisim. In the Shemoneh Esrei in the brocha of Modim during Maariv, and also in Birchas HaMazon, we add Al HaNisim, to thank Hashem for the miracle of Purim.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Megillah reading window (night and day)
verbatim: One may read the Megillah at night any time from tzeis hakochavim (7:42pm) – when the stars come out – until alos hashachar (5:34am) – rising of the morning star... The Megillah may be read on the day of Purim itself any time between sunrise and sunset (this year between 6:53am and 7:15pm).

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - children encouraged at Megillah
verbatim: Children should be encouraged and trained to listen to the Megillah. It is customary to bring even small children to shule to hear the Megillah reading (if they won't disturb). Parents should supervise their children to pay attention to the Megillah and not disturb anyone...

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Megillah reading etiquette (brochos, noise, no microphone)
verbatim: Megillah reading is preceded by three brochos: al mikrah Megillah, she'asah nissim, and shehecheyanu... It is not acceptable to use a microphone for the Megillah reading. One cannot fulfil one's obligation of hearing the Megillah if it is heard via telephone, internet or other live hookup.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - refreshments and small seudah
verbatim: Refreshments will be provided after the evening Megillah reading. We eat a small Seudah on the night of Purim. It is proper to set the table with a tablecloth to emphasise its festive nature. Some do not eat meat in the evening, so as to not make a mistake by thinking that this is the real se'udas Purim.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Shacharis minyanim
verbatim: There will be two minyanim for Shacharis on Purim morning, starting at 6:15am and 7:00am respectively.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Megillah day readings
verbatim: The Megillah readings will start at approx 6:45am and 7:30am respectively. We continue wearing Rashi Tefilin during the reading.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Mishloach Manos
verbatim: Every person is to send on Purim Day at least two portions of different food to at least one other person of the same gender, to demonstrate friendship and brotherhood among the Jewish people...

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - Matanos L'Evyonim
verbatim: Every person is required to give gifts or money of at least a nominal sum to two poor people on Purim day specifically (not by night)... The gifts should be worth at least a Peruta (approx 20c).

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Purim Seudah timing (Friday - before Chatzos)
time: before Chatzos HaYom 1:04pm; if delayed until 10th halachic hour 4:09pm
verbatim: This year however, since Purim is on a Friday, it is customary to make the Purim seudah before Chatzos HaYom (1:04pm), in honour of the upcoming Shabbos. In this way one will be able to eat the Shabbos evening meal properly with an appetite. If delayed, the Purim seudah can be until the 10th halachic hour (4:09pm). If delayed further, it can be until Shabbos comes in, but that is the least desirable.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana
verbatim: Sof Zeman Kiddush Levana for Adar this year is on Purim morning at 10:24am Sydney Daylight Saving time.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - early Mincha shifted due to Purim falling Friday
verbatim: Due to Purim day being a Friday this year, the time for the early Mincha will be earlier than usual. Kabbolas Shabbos and Maariv on Erev Shabbos will be according to the usual pattern for the early minyan and the regular minyan. Details are in the schedule.

week_context: Purim 5785 (2025) at Tzemach Tzedek - Notes, times, minhagim & halachos
label: NOTE - no Av Harachamim/Tzidkascha Tzedek
verbatim: We do not say Av Harachamim after the leining on Shabbos morning, nor do we say Tzidkascha Tzedek at Mincha on Shabbos.
section: Erev Shabbos and Shabbos Shushan Purim

verbatim: A person should add slightly to his joy and celebration during the meals of Shushan Purim. The Rebbe encouraged us to utilise the day of Shushan Purim to "make up" for anything that was missed out on Purim. Of course that is to be within a Shabbos context.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Nine Days - reduced joy from Rosh Chodesh Av
label: NOTE #1
verbatim: When the month of Av enters, we reduce our joy and minimise any business involvement that leads to joy.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #2
verbatim: Except for the clothes of young children, we don’t do any laundry, even if we will not be wearing the clothes until after Tisha b’Av.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Nine Days - daily siyum custom (Rosh Chodesh through 15 Av)
label: NOTE #3
verbatim: From Rosh Chodesh, up to and including 15 Menachem Av, it is customary to make or hear a siyum each day.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Nine Days - no meat or wine
label: NOTE #4
verbatim: During the Nine Days, we don’t eat meat or drink wine, except on Shabbos or at a Seudas Mitzvah.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Erev Shabbos Chazon - hot water/mikveh allowance
label: NOTE #5
verbatim: On Erev Shabbos Chazon (Friday 17 July), a person who washes with hot water and/or toivels himself in the mikveh every Friday may wash with hot water without soap (others do permit soap) and toivel himself on this Friday as well.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Shabbos Chazon - Alter Rebbe custom, Pirkei Avos ch.2
label: NOTE #6
verbatim: On Shabbos Chazon, the Alter Rebbe would wear all his customary Shabbos  clothing, without any deviation from his usual pattern. After Mincha we say the second chapter of Pirkei Avos.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Erev Tisha b'Av - Torah study restrictions from Chatzos, no Tachanun at Mincha
label: NOTE #7
verbatim: On Erev Tisha b’Av (Wednesday 22 July), all the restrictions that apply to Torah study on Tisha b’Av apply from Chatzot Hayom (12:01pm) onwards. We may study only the books of Eichah and Iyov, the unfavourable prophecies in the book of Yirmeyahu, Midrash Eichah and the description of the churban as recorded in the Talmud  Maseches Gittin 55b. At Mincha we do not say Tachanun.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #8
verbatim: At the meal before the Fast, we do not eat two cooked dishes. The prevailing custom is to eat a hard-boiled egg dipped in ashes. We do not drink any alcoholic drinks. Eating and drinking must be stopped by the time of Shkia.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Erev Tisha b'Av - Shkia, change footwear, Megillas Eichah/Kinos
label: NOTE #9
verbatim: At Shkia on Wednesday (5:09pm) we remove our leather shoes and put on non-leather footwear. For Maariv we sit on low chairs or cushions. Bring your own to shul. After Maariv we read Megillas Eichah and selected Kinos, then say veAtah Kadosh, a full kaddish, Aleinu and Kiddush Yatom.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Tisha b'Av - shiva mourner attendance allowance
label: NOTE #10
verbatim: A person who is sitting shiva (chas v’shalom) may be in shul in the evening and morning until the Kinos are finished, because we are all in mourning on this day.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Tisha b'Av morning - washing restriction on rising
label: NOTE #11
verbatim: Tisha b’Av: daytime.  Upon rising from sleep, a person should wash only his fingers, not his entire hand. If he has sleep around his eyes, and he would normally wash it off, he may do so on this occasion as well. We do not say the brocha “she’asah li kal tzarki” until the next morning.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #12
verbatim: We do not greet one another on Tisha b’Av.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Tisha b'Av Shacharis - low chair, no tallis/tefillin, Kinos, Eichah
label: NOTE #13
verbatim: Shacharis: sit on low chair or cushion – bring your own to shul. No tallis or tefillin are worn, and we do not kiss the tzizis of the tallis katan. We say Kinos after the Haftorah. It is customary for each person to say Megillas Eichah individually after Kinos.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Tisha b'Av - no work before Chatzos, food prep restriction
label: NOTE #14
verbatim: Until Chatzot Hayom (12:01pm) we don’t do any work that involves some time. Similarly we do not sit on regular chairs or prepare food for the evening meal until after Chatzot Hayom.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: Tisha b'Av - tallis/tefillin before Mincha, davening order
label: NOTE #15
verbatim: Before Mincha: we put on tallis and tefillin and say the appropriate brochos. Then the Shema, shir haYom, Ein Kelokeinu, the portion of Tehillim for the day and any shiurim which normally follow Shacharis. That concludes the omissions made in Shacharis. We then say the korbonos for Mincha, then continue with krias haTorah and Haftorah, followed by Shmonei Esrei etc.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #16
verbatim: We put on Rabbeinu Tam tefillin.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #17
verbatim: Ending the fast: We daven Maariv, wash negel vasser, wash our faces and rinse our mouths (no brocha).

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #18
verbatim: Make Kiddush Levanah.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #19
verbatim: We can wear leather shoes again.

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
label: NOTE #20
verbatim: Breaking the fast of Tisha b’Av:  Refreshments at Tzemach Tzedek after Maariv, with a contribution by Gershon & Yona Gunsberger in honour of those members of their families who died al kiddush Hashem during the Shoah, and also in honour of Gershon’s alte-zeida Reb Yisroel ben Avraham Kalman haLevi Z”L, whose yahrzeit is on Tisha b’Av .

week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
occasion: 10 Av - meat/wine restriction continues until Chatzos
label: NOTE #21
verbatim: We do not eat meat or drink wine until after Chatzot Hayom on 10 Av (this year at 12:01pm). Similarly the other restrictions of the Nine Days remain in place until after Chatzot Hayom.
